name: Copilot Prompts Generate Quality- and Error-conditioned Synthetic Student Inputs
statement: Watts et al. report using Microsoft Copilot 365 prompts with high-quality examples, low-quality examples, and typo-conditioned mixed examples to generate synthetic student inputs for chatbot prompt evaluation.
population_or_context: Middle-school scientific argumentation chatbot prototype evaluation.
quantitative_note: The source snippet reports 18 synthetic student inputs and 162 chatbot responses across prompt versions.
caveats: The generated text is chatbot input, not a scored student response corpus.
The finding is included for method taxonomy, not for automated scorer validation.
evaluation_method: Synthetic inputs were used to test multiple chatbot system prompts.
reported_metrics: 18 synthetic inputs; three independent chatbot responses for each input and version.
limitations: The generation context is scientific argumentation chatbot design.
The staged source locator relies on public ETS/MDPI summary snippets and should receive full-text source-fidelity review before publication.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The staged finding paraphrases the reported Copilot prompt sets for high-quality, low-quality, and typographical-error synthetic inputs.
